title: VS Code Custom Agents (.agent.md)
abstract: VS Code custom agents (declared via .agent.md files) allow teams to package domain-specific knowledge, tool restrictions, and workflow instructions into reusable agent personas that live alongside the code.
why_we_care: Custom agents let us codify team practices (code review standards, architecture decisions, deployment procedures) into shareable, version-controlled agent definitions. This is foundational to our OLAF skills approach and enables consistent AI-assisted workflows across the team without each developer needing to craft complex prompts.
summary: VS Code now supports custom agents defined in .agent.md files — packaging instructions, tool access, and workflow logic into reusable AI personas that any team member can invoke from the Copilot chat.
description:
## Overview

VS Code custom agents are declared via `.agent.md` files (YAML frontmatter + markdown body) placed in `.github/agents/` or project roots. They define:

- **Instructions**: Behavioral guidelines and domain knowledge
- **Tool restrictions**: Which tools the agent can/cannot use
- **applyTo patterns**: File patterns the agent specializes in
- **Model preferences**: Preferred LLM model for the agent

## Use Cases

- Code review with team-specific standards
- Architecture analysis agents
- Migration assistants
- Documentation generators
- Security audit agents

## Current Maturity

Shipped in VS Code Insiders, becoming stable. Active development with frequent feature additions. Our team has built 30+ custom agents via the OLAF skills system.
